description: 55M, HTN, CKD 3a (eGFR 52), LDL 145, ASCVD risk 8.5%. Three-guideline convergence: USPSTF Grade C, ACC/AHA R4, and KDIGO statin-for-CKD all independently recommend statin therapy via shared medication entities. CKD context favors moderate intensity.

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: Three-guideline convergence on statin initiation: ACC/AHA COR I, LOE A for primary prevention (ASCVD risk ≥7.5%, LDL 70-189); USPSTF Grade C for shared decision-making (risk 7.5-<10%); KDIGO 1A for CKD statin therapy. CKD G3a (eGFR 52) context favors moderate intensity. CKD is a cardiovascular risk enhancer that strengthens the case for initiation.

actions.label: Monitor eGFR and urine ACR per KDIGO
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: regular monitoring of eGFR and urine ACR for CKD staging and progression tracking. CKD G3a (eGFR 52) with normal albuminuria (ACR 18) indicates moderate risk. Monitor at least annually.

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure management
actions.rationale: BP 140/86 mmHg is above target. HTN is both a CVD risk factor and an accelerator of CKD progression. Target BP <130/80 per KDIGO for CKD patients.
